title: Review: Ferrum language design
notes:
DX rating: AWKWARD — The effect system and allocation model are well-designed but the surface has too much notation debt: three dispatch syntaxes, two self-param forms, region annotations mixing two bracket systems, effect variables as a third generic mechanism. The common paths (write a struct, implement a trait, write an async function) are navigable but have more surface friction than necessary.
Confidence: 70% chance of missed design flaw. Least confident flags: (1) the self: &Self vs &self issue — I identified both forms in the spec but didn't determine if the spec defines both as valid syntax or if one is an error; (2) the Result[T] single-param form — there may be a default error type defined somewhere I didn't read (the spec is large). Would update assessment if: shown a section of the spec that defines a default error type for Result, or a section that explicitly allows both self: &Self and &self as equivalent syntax.
